results.rendered_html: rāmānuja m. ‘younger brother of Rāma’ (this title would be applicable to Kṛṣṇa as born after Bala-rāma of the same father)

results.rendered_html: N. of a celebrated Vaiṣṇava reformer (founder of a particular Vedantic school which taught the doctrine of viśiṣṭādvEta or qualified non-duality i.e. that the human spirit is separate and different from the one Supreme Spirit though dependent on it and ultimately to be united with it; he lived at Kāñcīpuram and Śrī-raṅgam in the South of India, in the 12th century, and is believed by his followers to have been an incarnation of Śeṣa; he is also called Rāmānujācārya and Yati-rāja; n. or ˚jam matam, Rāmānuja's doctrine),  RTL. 119, 448  &c.

results.rendered_html: rāmānuja (rāma + a˚) m. N. pr. eines berühmten Vedānta-Philosophen und Vaiṣṇava  WILSON, Sel. Works I, 34. fgg.   Verz. d. Tüb. H. 13 .  HALL 92 . 95 . 112 . 116 . 118 . 172 . 203 .  WEBER, RĀMAT. UP. 281. fg.  284 .  SARVADARŚANAS. 45,21 . 51,19 . 56,10 . 61,9 .  Verz. d. Oxf. H. 220,a, No. 527 . 221,b, No. 536 . 243,b, No. 604 . 299,b, No. 732 . 300,a, No. 733 . wohl adj. zu Rāmānuja in Beziehung stehend 285,b, No. 669 .
